title: FileHeader/FileChunk protocol types + extend pad buckets
description:
## What
Add FileHeader and FileChunk variants to ClearMessage enum in core/src/messages.rs. Extend pad() BUCKETS to include 65536 bytes (allows ~65KB padded chunks). Add serde_with Base64 encoding for FileChunk::data.

## Algorithm
1. In messages.rs, import use serde_with::{serde_as, Base64}; and add #[serde_with::serde_as] attribute to ClearMessage.
2. Add FileHeader { transfer_id: Uuid, name: String, size_bytes: u64, sha256_hex: String, total_chunks: u32, mime_type: String } variant.
3. Add FileChunk { transfer_id: Uuid, seq: u32, #[serde_as(as = 'Base64')] data: Vec<u8> } variant.
4. In pad(), change BUCKETS from [256, 512, 1024, 4096] to [256, 512, 1024, 4096, 65536]. Update error message to '65532-byte maximum'. Update pad_too_large_returns_err test to use 65533 bytes instead of 4093.
5. Add roundtrip tests: file_header_roundtrips, file_chunk_data_base64_in_json, file_chunk_roundtrips_binary_data.
6. Run cargo test -p nie-core.

## Defensive programming
- transfer_id Uuid is always client-generated (never relay-supplied)
- sha256_hex field is hex only (64 chars) - validated at receive side
- Never log chunk data bytes
- name field: validated at receive side before writing to disk (path traversal check)
- seq is 0-indexed, total_chunks is the count

## Tests
- file_header_roundtrips: serialize FileHeader, deserialize, assert all fields match (oracle: serde_json)
- file_chunk_data_base64_in_json: serialize FileChunk with binary data, assert JSON contains 'data' field as base64 string not array (oracle: base64 spec - binary round-trips through base64)
- file_chunk_roundtrips_binary_data: roundtrip FileChunk with 48000 bytes of random-ish data, assert data preserved
- pad_accepts_65532_bytes: pad with 65532-byte input, assert output is 65536 bytes, unpad recovers original
- pad_rejects_65533_bytes: assert pad([0u8; 65533]) returns Err
acceptance_criteria: cargo test -p nie-core passes; FileHeader and FileChunk serialize/deserialize correctly; pad(65532 bytes) produces 65536-byte output
close_reason: FileHeader/FileChunk variants added, pad() extended to 65536 bucket, all match arms updated across codebase, 108 core tests pass
